  1491→- [ ] Isolation subshells (`( ... ) || buc_die`) have `|| buc_die` on every internal command and on the outer boundary
  1492→
  1493→### Command Substitution Rules
  1494→- [ ] NO command substitution except `$(<file)` builtin and `_capture` functions
  1495→- [ ] Temp files used instead of complex command substitution
  1496→- [ ] `$(<file)` always followed by validation
  1497→- [ ] `_capture` functions properly named with suffix
  1498→
  1499→### Loop Safety

1500→- [ ] All while-read loops use load-then-iterate pattern
  1501→- [ ] All `while read` conditions include trailing-newline guard: `|| test -n "${z_var}"`
  1502→- [ ] No file descriptor held open across function calls that may touch stdin
  1503→
  1504→### Bash Compatibility
  1505→- [ ] No bash 4+ features (associative arrays, `**`, readarray)
  1506→- [ ] Use `test` not `[[ ]]` except for pattern matching `=~`
  1507→- [ ] Use `=` not `==` in test expressions
  1508→- [ ] No HERE documents (heredocs)
  1509→- [ ] Here-strings (`<<<`) single-line piping only; no multi-line
  1510→- [ ] Target bash 3.2 minimum
  1511→- [ ] Array iteration uses index pattern for `set -u` safety
  1512→
  1513→### Naming Conventions
  1514→- [ ] Module prefix: 2-4 lowercase letters + underscore
  1515→- [ ] Public functions: `«prefix»_«command»` (snake_case)
  1516→- [ ] Internal functions: `z«prefix»_«name»` (snake_case)
  1517→- [ ] Internal constants: `Z«PREFIX»_«NAME»` (SCREAMING_SNAKE)
  1518→- [ ] Public constants: `«PREFIX»_«NAME»` (SCREAMING_SNAKE)
  1519→- [ ] Special functions: `*_predicate`, `*_capture`, `*_enroll`, `*_recite` suffixes
  1520→
  1521→### Documentation & Visibility
  1522→- [ ] Public functions documented with `buc_doc_*` blocks
  1523→- [ ] Internal functions documented with `buc_doc_*` blocks (recommended)
  1524→- [ ] `buc_doc_shown || return 0` after documentation in functions that have it
  1525→- [ ] Runtime info via `buc_log_«source»` (forensic) and `buc_step` (milestones), never comments
  1526→- [ ] Details use `buc_log_«source»` (transcript.txt)
  1527→- [ ] Comments only for code clarification, not runtime info
  1528→- [ ] No commit-message comments (narrating the change, not the code)
  1529→- [ ] Major operations use `buc_step` (always visible)
  1530→
  1531→### Sourcing Restrictions
  1532→- [ ] CLI header sources only `buc_command.sh`
  1533→- [ ] `z«prefix»_furnish` sources all other dependencies and config files
  1534→- [ ] Furnish gates with `buc_doc_env_done || return 0` before any sourcing
  1535→- [ ] Credential sourcing documented and never writes to disk
  1536→- [ ] No other sourcing anywhere
  1537→
  1538→### Interface Contamination Discipline
  1539→- [ ] Commands accept exactly one canonical form for each argument — no aliases
  1540→- [ ] No case folding, prefix stripping, or normalization of inputs
  1541→- [ ] No `${1:-default}` for required parameters — missing required args cause `buc_die`
  1542→- [ ] `buc_doc_*` output shows the single canonical invocation form
  1543→- [ ] Zeroes theory applied: no unexplained state space expansion
  1544→
  1545→### Code Quality
  1546→- [ ] Prefer bash builtins over external tools (parameter expansion vs sed/awk)
  1547→- [ ] Use BCU utilities instead of raw bash (buc_die vs echo+exit)
  1548→- [ ] Use enrollment for config validation, `buv_dir_exists`/`buv_file_exists` for path checks
  1549→- [ ] Consistent error messages (specific and actionable)
  1550→- [ ] Proper temp file naming with module prefix
  1551→- [ ] No silent failures or ignored conditions
  1552→- [ ] No `2>/dev/null` — stderr redirected to temp file, path included in `buc_die` message
  1553→- [ ] Temp files never deleted in module code — preserved for forensics
  1554→
  1555→### Enterprise Safety
  1556→- [ ] Crash-fast principle applied throughout
  1557→- [ ] No elegant-but-fragile pipeline patterns
  1558→- [ ] Secrets never written to disk (use `_capture` functions)
  1559→- [ ] Temp files make failures visible and debuggable
  1560→- [ ] Every potential error explicitly handled
  1561→- [ ] Abstraction layers used (BCU utilities, enrollment infrastructure)
  1562→
  1563→### Regime Archetype (when applicable)
  1564→- [ ] Kindle calls `buv_*_enroll` for each config field
  1565→- [ ] Kindle calls `buv_scope_sentinel` after all enrollments, before `KINDLED=1`
  1566→- [ ] `z«prefix»_enforce` calls `buv_vet SCOPE` then custom format checks
  1567→- [ ] Furnish calls kindle → enforce (ironclad gate)
  1568→- [ ] CLI provides `*_validate` command using `buv_report`
  1569→- [ ] CLI provides `*_render` command using `buv_render`
  1570→
  1571→---
  1572→
  1573→## Test Execution Patterns
  1574→
  1575→### Principled Subshell Use
  1576→
  1577→BCG prohibits subshells for error handling (see **Subshell Exit vs Brace-Group**). Test execution is the principled counterpoint: subshells provide **isolation boundaries** that prevent state leakage between fixtures and cases.
  1578→
  1579→In bash 3.2, there is no module system, no scope isolation, and no cleanup-on-exit guarantee. When fixture A kindles modules and fixture B kindles the same modules, the kindle guards (`Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED`) from fixture A block fixture B. Subshells solve this: all state — kindle guards, sourced functions, variables — dies at the `)` boundary.
  1580→
  1581→This is discipline encoded as structure.
  1582→
  1583→### Three-Layer Model
  1584→
  1585→Test execution uses three layers separated by two subshell boundaries:
  1586→
  1587→```
  1588→Parent Shell (runner layer)
  1589→ ├─ _litmus_predicate — precondition check (0=proceed, 1=skip)
  1590→ ├─ Fixture iteration and status tracking
  1591→ └─ Reporting (pass/fail/skip counts)
  1592→     │
  1593→     └─ Fixture Subshell (fixture boundary)
  1594→         ├─ _baste — kindle, source, configure
  1595→         ├─ Fixture temp dir creation
  1596→         └─ Case iteration
  1597→             │
  1598→             └─ Case Subshell (_tcase boundary)
  1599→                 ├─ Per-case BUT_TEMP_DIR and BUTE_BURV_ROOT

1600→                 ├─ Assertions and verification
  1601→                 └─ Exit status communicated to fixture
  1602→```
  1603→
  1604→### Vocabulary
  1605→
  1606→**Fixture boundary** — the fixture isolation boundary:
  1607→- Runs in a subshell (state dies at boundary)
  1608→- Baste runs inside (visible to cases, dies with fixture)
  1609→- Litmus runs outside (parent decides whether to enter)
  1610→- Communicates only exit status to the runner
  1611→- Kindle guards catch double-kindle within a fixture; subshell boundary prevents cross-fixture contamination
  1612→
  1613→**`_tcase`** — the case isolation boundary:
  1614→- Runs in a subshell within the fixture subshell
  1615→- Inherits baste state, cannot mutate sibling state
  1616→- Communicates only exit status and stdio
  1617→- Each case gets isolated temp dir and BURV root
  1618→
  1619→**`_litmus_predicate`** — fixture precondition (parent layer):
  1620→- Runs in parent shell, outside the fixture boundary
  1621→- Returns 0 to proceed, 1 to skip fixture
  1622→- Must not kindle or source modules — state would persist to next fixture
  1623→- Reusable: multiple fixtures share one litmus; composable: one litmus calls others
  1624→- Registered as second argument to `butr_fixture_enroll`
  1625→
  1626→**`_baste`** — fixture preparation function (fixture layer):
  1627→- Runs inside the fixture subshell
  1628→- Kindles modules, sources dependencies, sets configuration
  1629→- State visible to all cases within this fixture, dies at fixture boundary
  1630→- Registered as third argument to `butr_fixture_enroll`
  1631→
  1632→### Allowed Operations by Layer
  1633→
  1634→| Operation | Parent | Fixture | `_tcase` |
  1635→|-----------|--------|---------|----------|
  1636→| Litmus predicate check | ✅ | — | — |
  1637→| `z«prefix»_kindle` | — | ✅ | — |
  1638→| `source` module files | — | ✅ | — |
  1639→| Baste configuration | — | ✅ | — |
  1640→| Case iteration | — | ✅ | — |
  1641→| Assertions (`buto_*_expect_*`) | — | — | ✅ |
  1642→| `zbuto_invoke` (capture) | — | — | ✅ |
  1643→| Tabtarget invocation | — | — | ✅ |
  1644→| Status tracking (pass/fail/skip) | ✅ | — | — |
  1645→
  1646→### Communication Across Boundaries
  1647→
  1648→| Boundary | Crosses | Does Not Cross |
  1649→|----------|---------|----------------|
  1650→| Fixture → parent | Exit status (0=pass, non-zero=fail, 2=skip), stdout/stderr | Variables, kindle state, sourced functions |
  1651→| `_tcase` → fixture | Exit status (0=pass, non-zero=fail), stdout/stderr | Variable mutations, BURV roots, temp dirs |
  1652→
  1653→### Naming Conventions
  1654→
  1655→| Element | Pattern | Example |
  1656→|---------|---------|---------|
  1657→| Testbench file | `«prefix»tb_testbench.sh` | `rbtb_testbench.sh` |
  1658→| Test case file | `«prefix»tc«xx»_«Name».sh` | `rbtckk_KickTires.sh` |
  1659→| Litmus predicate | `z«tb»_«name»_litmus_predicate` | `zrbtb_container_runtime_litmus_predicate` |
  1660→| Baste function | `z«tb»_«name»_baste` | `zrbtb_ark_baste` |
  1661→| Case function | `«tc»_«name»_tcase` | `rbtckk_false_tcase` |
  1662→| Fixture enrollment | `butr_fixture_enroll` | — |
  1663→| Suite enrollment | `butr_suite_enroll` | Sets sweep suite context |
  1664→| Case enrollment | `butr_case_enroll` | — |
  1665→
  1666→### Compliance Checking
  1667→
  1668→The naming conventions enable grep-based auditing:
  1669→
  1670→```bash
  1671→# Find all litmus predicates
  1672→grep -rn '_litmus_predicate' Tools/
  1673→
  1674→# Find all baste functions
  1675→grep -rn '_baste' Tools/
  1676→
  1677→# Find all case functions
  1678→grep -rn '_tcase' Tools/
  1679→
  1680→# Verify enrollment matches naming — litmus/baste/case functions should
  1681→# appear both in enrollment calls and as function definitions
  1682→grep -rn 'butr_fixture_enroll\|butr_case_enroll' Tools/
  1683→```
  1684→
  1685→---
  1686→
  1687→## Shellcheck Integration
  1688→
  1689→BCG uses [shellcheck](https://www.shellcheck.net) for static analysis of bash source with a curated suppress list that eliminates false positives caused by BCG's module architecture.
  1690→
  1691→### Architecture
  1692→
  1693→| Artifact | Location | Purpose |
  1694→|----------|----------|---------|
  1695→| `busc_shellcheckrc` | `Tools/buk/` | BCG-structural suppressions with rationale |
  1696→| `buq_cli.sh` | `Tools/buk/` | BUK qualification CLI hosting shellcheck command |
  1697→| `buw-qsc` | Workbench route | Invokes `buq_shellcheck` |
  1698→| `buw-qsc.QualifyShellCheck.sh` | `tt/` | Tabtarget for shellcheck qualification |
  1699→

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive view of the BCG. Let me compile a clear summary of all the key compliance rules and patterns.

## BCG (Bash Console Guide) - Complete Compliance Rules Summary

Here are all the key rules and patterns that bash code in this project must follow:

### CORE PHILOSOPHY
- **Enterprise bash first**: Boring, reliably correct code optimized for 3am debugging
- **Crash-fast principle**: Every error handled at the point of failure, never at distance
- **No elegant pipelines**: Temp files make each step visible, testable, and recoverable
- **No traps/signal handlers**: Error handling lives where failures happen
- **Bash 3.2 compatibility**: Reject all bash 4+ features (no associative arrays, `**` globbing, etc.)

---

### 1. VARIABLE NAMING CONVENTIONS

| Variable Type | Pattern | Case | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Module prefix | `prefix_` | 2-4 lowercase letters | e.g., `rbv_`, `buc_` |
| **Kindle constants (internal)** | `Z«PREFIX»_NAME` | SCREAMING_SNAKE | Always `readonly` |
| **Kindle constants (public)** | `«PREFIX»_NAME` | SCREAMING_SNAKE | Always `readonly` |
| **Literal constants (public)** | `«PREFIX»_name` | lower_snake | Pure literals only, no `${}` |
| **Mutable kindle state** | `z_«prefix»_name` | lowercase, NO readonly | Counters, rolls, builder state |
| **Local parameters** | `z_name` | lowercase | Always use `z_` prefix |
| **Roll arrays** | `z_«prefix»_name_roll` | lowercase | Parallel arrays, kindle-only |
| **Enroll return vars** | `z_«funcname»_«retval»` | lowercase | Function name verbatim |

**Key Rule**: NEVER mix literal constants with kindle constants. Literal constants have NO `${}` expansion and no runtime dependency — they're available immediately after sourcing. Kindle constants are computed at runtime during kindle.

---

### 2. ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS

#### Command Failures
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Every command that fails needs explicit handling
command || buc_die "Failed to do X — see ${z_stderr_file}"

# ✅ For predicates and capture functions (never die)
command || return 1

# ❌ NEVER: Silent failures
command 2>/dev/null
```

#### Stderr Capture (Never Suppress)
```bash
# ❌ NEVER suppress stderr
curl -sS "${z_url}" 2>/dev/null

# ✅ ALWAYS capture stderr to temp file, reference in die message
curl -sS "${z_url}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "Failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"

# In loops, use auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness
local z_index=0
while ...; do
  command 2>"${Z«PREFIX»_PREFIX}${z_index}_stderr.txt" || buc_die "..."
  z_index=$((z_index + 1))
done
```

#### Test Expressions
```bash
# ✅ ALWAYS use test command with || handling
test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "File not found"
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_die "Parameter required"
test "${z_var}" = "expected" || buc_warn "Unexpected"

# ❌ NEVER use test with command substitution
test $(echo "${z_val}" | grep pattern)  # ❌ Wrong

# ✅ Use exit-status predicates instead
echo "${z_val}" | grep -qE '^pattern$' || buc_die "Invalid"

# ✅ Or use case statement for patterns
case "${z_val}" in
  *pattern*) buc_log_args "Found pattern" ;;
  *) buc_die "Pattern not found" ;;
esac
```

#### Control Flow After Tests (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ✅ ALWAYS invert test and use || (BCG standard)
test "${z_val}" != "done" || break
test -n "${z_val}" || return 0
[[ "${z_line}" != *"pattern"* ]] || continue

# ❌ NEVER use test ... && for control flow
test "${z_val}" = "done" && break  # Wrong!

# ❌ NEVER use A && B || C pseudo-ternary
curl "${z_url}" && jq '.field' "${z_file}" || buc_die  # Wrong!

# ✅ Use explicit if/then/else instead
if curl "${z_url}"; then
  jq '.field' "${z_file}" || buc_die "jq failed"
else
  buc_die "curl failed"
fi
```

#### `set -e` Is Not Sufficient
```bash
# POSIX rule: set -e is SUPPRESSED inside if/while/||/&& test expressions
# This suppression PROPAGATES through the entire call tree

# ❌ WRONG: set -e suppressed, failures in some_function invisible
if some_function; then ...

# ✅ REQUIRED: Only _predicate functions in conditionals
if z«prefix»_ready_predicate; then ...

# ✅ REQUIRED: Regular functions use explicit error handling
some_function || buc_die "Failed"
```

#### Brace-Group vs Subshell for Error Handling
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Subshell exit only terminates subshell, not script
some_cmd || (echo "ERROR" >&2 && exit 1)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Brace-group stays in same process
some_cmd || { echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1; }
```

---

### 3. FUNCTION NAMING PATTERNS

| Type | Pattern | First Line | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Boilerplate kindle** | `z«prefix»_kindle` | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" \|\| buc_die` | Define kindle constants, set module state |
| **Boilerplate sentinel** | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | Guard all functions |
| **Boilerplate enforce** | `z«prefix»_enforce` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Validate regime (if applicable) |
| **Public functions** | `«prefix»_«command»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Always starts with sentinel check |
| **Internal helpers** | `z«prefix»_«name»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Prefix with `z` |
| **Predicate** | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_predicate` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Returns 0/1, no output, never dies |
| **Capture** | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_capture` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Returns stdout once or exit 1, no stderr |
| **Enroll** | `[z]«prefix»_[«scope»_]enroll` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Mutates rolls, returns via `z_«funcname»_«retval»` vars, kindle-only |
| **Recite** | `[z]«prefix»_«what»_recite` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Read-only roll access, outputs via echo |

**Special Function Rules**:
- **Predicate**: Never dies, status only (0=true, 1=false), no output, use in conditionals
- **Capture**: Clean error handling, single return via echo, no secrets on disk, use two-line pattern
- **Enroll**: Populate parallel arrays (rolls), return via variables not echo, kindle-only
- **Recite**: Read-only access to rolls, safe to call in `$()`

---

### 4. FILE I/O PATTERNS (ZEROES THEORY)

#### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Nested command substitution hides failures
z_result=$(echo $(complex_command) | process)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Temp files make failures visible
complex_command > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex failed"
process < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
read -r z_result < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "No result"

# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: Secrets use _capture functions, never temp files
```

**Temp file lifecycle**: Files under `BURD_TEMP_DIR` are **preserved after execution** for forensic debugging. Never delete them in module code.

#### File Reading Pattern
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Single-line builtin with validation
local -r z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"

# ❌ NEVER: Split declaration from $(<file) — unnecessary verbosity
local z_content
z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")
```

#### Capture Secrets (Never Disk)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Capture pattern for secrets
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"

# ❌ NEVER: Split declaration with capture
local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!
```

#### Line Reading from Files
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Guard handles missing trailing newline
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo"
done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"

# ✅ REQUIRED: Load-then-iterate pattern for loops (prevents stdin consumption)
local z_items=()
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  z_items+=("${z_line}")
done < "${z_temp_file}"

# ✅ Now iterate — stdin is free
for z_item in "${z_items[@]}"; do
  complex_function "${z_item}"
done
```

**Critical**: Never read file while loop body is open (stdin consumption). Load all items first, then iterate.

---

### 5. SUBSHELL USAGE

#### Error-Handling Blocks (FORBIDDEN)
```bash
# ❌ FORBIDDEN: Error blocks must use brace-group, not subshell
some_cmd || (echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use brace-group
some_cmd || { echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1; }
```

#### Legitimate Subshell Uses
```bash
# ✅ Isolation subshells (environment containment)
(
  source "${z_config_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source"
  «prefix»_operation || buc_die "Operation failed"
) || buc_die "Isolation subshell failed"

# ✅ Test execution (principled isolation)
# ✅ $() command substitution inside _capture functions
# ✅ Any sequence where environment mutations must not persist
```

---

### 6. LOCAL VARIABLES AND ASSIGNMENT

#### Variable Expansion (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Always use braced, quoted expansion
"${z_var}"              # Prevents word splitting, glob expansion
"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
"${HOME}/path"          # Use ${HOME} not ~

# ❌ NEVER use bare or unbraced expansion
$var                    # Unquoted — word splitting risk
"$var"                  # Unbraced — boundary ambiguity
${var}                  # Unquoted — glob expansion risk
~/path                  # Tilde in quotes doesn't expand
```

#### Local Variable Declaration
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: One declaration per line with explicit initialization
local z_name=""
local z_value="default"
local z_result=0

# ❌ NEVER: Multiple declarations on one line
local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use local -r for single-assignment locals (majority of locals)
local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
local -r z_content=$(<"${z_file}")

# EXCEPTIONS to local -r (these need mutable):
# 1. Loop counters
local z_index=0
z_index=$((z_index + 1))

# 2. Per-iteration synthesized variables
local z_manifest_file=""
for z_spec in "${z_specs[@]}"; do
  z_manifest_file="${Z_PREFIX}${z_index}_manifest.json"
done

# 3. Two-line capture pattern (readonly would lock empty value)
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed"

# 4. Accumulators and state-tracking flags
local z_any_failed=0
local z_current_group=""

# ❌ NEVER: local -i (silently coerces non-integers to 0)
local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0!

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use plain local with explicit validation
local z_count="${z_input}"
test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "Must be non-negative int"
```

#### Parameter Handling
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Defensive parameter pattern with defaults
local -r z_param1="${1:-}"
local -r z_param2="${2:-}"
test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "param1 is required"

# ❌ NEVER: Silent defaults for required parameters
local -r z_param="${1:-default}"  # Silently uses default if missing
```

---

### 7. READONLY PATTERNS (Three Scopes)

#### `readonly Z«PREFIX»_NAME` — Kindle Constants
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: All kindle assignments must be readonly
z«prefix»_kindle() {
  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "already kindled"
  
  # Literal constants
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_API_VERSION="v2"
  
  # Computed from other variables
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_LOCATION}${RBGC_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  
  # Composed from prior kindle assignments
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_ACCEPT_TYPES="${Z«PREFIX»_TYPE_A},${Z«PREFIX»_TYPE_B}"
  
  # MUST be last — sentinel checks this to know kindle is complete
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}

# ❌ NEVER: Kindle constant outside kindle
z«prefix»_setup() {
  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"  # VIOLATION
}
```

#### `local -r` — Default for Local Variables
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Majority of locals use local -r
local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
local -r z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"

# Exceptions: Loop counters, per-iteration vars, two-line capture, accumulators
```

#### `readonly VAR` — Lock After Enforce (Regime Archetype)
```bash
# After z«prefix»_enforce succeeds, lock enrolled variables
readonly REGI_FIELD_ONE REGI_FIELD_TWO REGI_FIELD_THREE

# Build derived state from validated values after lock
Z«PREFIX»_DOCKER_ENV=("-e" "REGI_FIELD_ONE=${REGI_FIELD_ONE}")
```

---

### 8. CONDITIONAL LOGIC AND BRANCHING

#### Use `test` Not `[[ ]]`
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use test command for simple tests
test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "File not found"
test "${z_var}" = "expected" || buc_warn "Unexpected"
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_step "Running with zero-length parameter"

# ✅ EXCEPTION: Pattern matching ONLY
[[ "${z_filename}" =~ \.tar\.gz$ ]] && z_extension="tar.gz"

# ❌ NEVER: [[ ]] for simple tests
[[ -f "${z_file}" ]]        # Use: test -f
[[ "${z_var}" == "value" ]] # Use: test ... =

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use = not == for POSIX compliance
test "${z_var}" = "value"   # Not ==
```

#### Array Iteration (Under `set -u`)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Index iteration (always safe on empty arrays)
for z_i in "${!z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do
  echo "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[$z_i]}" || buc_die "Failed"
done

# ✅ REQUIRED: Guarded value iteration (guard prevents expansion)
if (( ${#z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]} )); then
  for z_val in "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do
    echo "${z_val}" || buc_die "Failed"
  done
fi

# ❌ NEVER: Unguarded value iteration on possibly-empty array
for z_val in "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do  # Crashes if empty!
  echo "${z_val}"
done
```

#### HERE-Strings (Not HERE-Docs)
```bash
# ✅ ALLOWED: Single-line HERE-string for piping variables
jq '.field' <<<"${z_json_data}"
grep "pattern" <<<"${z_content}"

# ❌ FORBIDDEN: Multi-line HERE-strings and HERE-docs
cat <<EOF > "${z_file}"
multi-line
content
EOF
```

---

### 9. INTERFACE CONTAMINATION DISCIPLINE

**One canonical form, no tolerances.**

```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: One canonical form for each parameter
«prefix»_command --option value

# ❌ NEVER: Accept alternatives
--option value      # Don't also accept -o
value               # Don't also accept positional
--OPTION value      # Don't also accept case-folded variants

# ✅ REQUIRED: Document canonical form only
buc_doc_param "--option" "Description of --option"

# ❌ NEVER: Undocumented defaults for required args
local -r z_param="${1:-default}"  # Silently fills missing required args

# ✅ REQUIRED: Explicit validation for required args
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_die "Parameter required"

# ❌ NEVER: Silent normalization
# Don't strip prefixes, fold case, or accept aliases
```

---

### 10. MISCELLANEOUS PATTERNS

#### Bare File Truncation
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Looks like accidental deletion
> "${z_file}"

# ✅ REQUIRED: Explicit no-op makes intent unambiguous
: > "${z_file}"
```

#### Arithmetic
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use $((...)) bash builtin
z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))

# ❌ NEVER: Use expr or bc
z_result=$(expr $z_value \* 2 + 10)
```

#### String Manipulation
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Prefer parameter expansion (bash builtin)
"${z_path##*/}"                    # basename
"${z_path%/*}"                     # dirname
"${z_string//old/new}"             # sed 's/old/new/g'
"${z_string#prefix}"               # sed 's/^prefix//'
"${z_string%suffix}"               # sed 's/suffix$//'

# ✅ REQUIRED: When using %/%% with variables, quote inner expansion
"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"          # Correct
"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"          # Correct

# ❌ NEVER: Unquoted inner expansion
"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"            # Pattern matched as glob!
```

#### Bash Builtins Preferred Over External Tools
```bash
# ✅ String manipulation, arithmetic, parameter expansion use builtins
# ✅ This reduces process spawns, potential failures, and overhead
```

---

### 11. MODULE STRUCTURE

#### CLI as Module Gateway (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: External code calls z*_kindle directly
source "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_bottle.sh"
zrbob_kindle                          # VIOLATION

# ✅ REQUIRED: All external access goes through CLI
exec "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_cli.sh" rbob_start "$@"

# External code = entry points, workbenches, testbenches, dispatchers
# They must use CLI scripts, not direct kindle calls
```

#### CLI Template
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: CLI header
set -euo pipefail
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

z«prefix»_furnish() {
  # Document env vars actually used
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR" "..."
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
  
  # Source dependencies
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/«prefix»_«name».sh"
  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed"
  
  z«prefix»_kindle
  z«prefix»_enforce  # If regime archetype
}

buc_execute «prefix»_ "description" z«prefix»_furnish "$@"
```

#### Implementation Module Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Copyright

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module multiply sourced"
Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no $)
«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions

z«prefix»_kindle() {
  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Already kindled"
  
  # Validate BURD variables used
  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  
  # Kindle constants
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}

z«prefix»_sentinel() {
  test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Not kindled"
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions

«prefix»_«command»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  local -r z_param1="${1:-}"
  
  buc_doc_brief "Description"
  buc_doc_param "arg" "description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "Param required"
  buc_step "User-visible milestone"
  buc_log_args "Detailed progress"
  
  command || buc_die "Failed"
  buc_success "Success message"
}
```

---

### 12. DOCUMENTATION AND VISIBILITY

#### Never Use Comments for Runtime Info
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use runtime visibility functions
buc_step "Fetching manifest from registry"         # User milestones
buc_log_args "Processing platform linux/amd64"     # Variable details
jq '.response' file.json | buc_log_pipe            # Pipeline output

# ❌ NEVER: Use comments for runtime information
# Building JWT header      ❌ Wrong
buc_log_args "Building JWT header"  ✅ Correct

# ❌ NEVER: Commit-message comments (narrate change, not code)
# Added qualification subsystem for shellcheck support  ❌ Wrong
buc_code "command"  ✅ Show commands with buc_code
```

#### Function Documentation (buc_doc_*)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Document all public functions
«prefix»_«command»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  
  buc_doc_brief "One-line description"
  buc_doc_param "required_arg" "Description"
  buc_doc_oparm "optional_arg" "Description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  # Implementation
}

# ✅ RECOMMENDED: Document internal functions too
z«prefix»_«name»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  
  buc_doc_brief "One-line description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  # Implementation
}
```

---

### 13. PROHIBITED CONSTRUCTS

| Construct | Why Forbidden | Alternative |
|---|---|---|
| **Inline shellcheck directives** | All suppression in busc_shellcheckrc | Configure centrally |
| **Bash 4+ features** | Bash 3.2 compatibility | Use 3.2-compatible alternatives |
| **Associative arrays** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use parallel arrays with indexes |
| **`**` globbing** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use find or explicit patterns |
| **readarray/mapfile** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use while/read loop |
| **HERE-docs** | Breaks indentation | Use echo sequences or temp files |
| **`eval` for assignment** | Quoting hazards, injection | Use `printf -v` or `${!var}` |
| **`test $(command)`** | Crashes when command returns nothing | Use `command -q` or `case` |
| **`test ... && break/return`** | Relies on set -e exemption hazard | Invert test, use `\|\|` |
| **Regular functions in conditionals** | set -e suppressed entire call tree | Only `_predicate` in if/while |
| **Subshell error handling** | exit only kills subshell | Use brace-group `{ ... }` |

---

### 14. QUICK VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

- [ ] All variables use `"${var}"` pattern (braced, quoted)
- [ ] All kindle constants are `readonly`
- [ ] Mutable kindle state uses lowercase `z_` prefix
- [ ] Every command that fails has `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`
- [ ] Stderr redirected to temp file, path in die message
- [ ] Control flow uses `||` not `&&` with tests
- [ ] Only `_predicate` functions in `if`/`while` conditions
- [ ] Error blocks use `{ ... }` not `( ... )`
- [ ] No bash 4+ features (associative arrays, `**`, readarray)
- [ ] No bare `> file` truncation (use `: > file`)
- [ ] No inline shellcheck directives
- [ ] No commit-message comments
- [ ] File reads validated for non-empty
- [ ] Two-line capture pattern for substitutions
- [ ] Temp files preserved for forensics (never deleted)

This is the complete BCG compliance framework for this project.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive view of the BCG. Let me compile a clear summary of all the key compliance rules and patterns.

## BCG (Bash Console Guide) - Complete Compliance Rules Summary

Here are all the key rules and patterns that bash code in this project must follow:

### CORE PHILOSOPHY
- **Enterprise bash first**: Boring, reliably correct code optimized for 3am debugging
- **Crash-fast principle**: Every error handled at the point of failure, never at distance
- **No elegant pipelines**: Temp files make each step visible, testable, and recoverable
- **No traps/signal handlers**: Error handling lives where failures happen
- **Bash 3.2 compatibility**: Reject all bash 4+ features (no associative arrays, `**` globbing, etc.)

---

### 1. VARIABLE NAMING CONVENTIONS

| Variable Type | Pattern | Case | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Module prefix | `prefix_` | 2-4 lowercase letters | e.g., `rbv_`, `buc_` |
| **Kindle constants (internal)** | `Z«PREFIX»_NAME` | SCREAMING_SNAKE | Always `readonly` |
| **Kindle constants (public)** | `«PREFIX»_NAME` | SCREAMING_SNAKE | Always `readonly` |
| **Literal constants (public)** | `«PREFIX»_name` | lower_snake | Pure literals only, no `${}` |
| **Mutable kindle state** | `z_«prefix»_name` | lowercase, NO readonly | Counters, rolls, builder state |
| **Local parameters** | `z_name` | lowercase | Always use `z_` prefix |
| **Roll arrays** | `z_«prefix»_name_roll` | lowercase | Parallel arrays, kindle-only |
| **Enroll return vars** | `z_«funcname»_«retval»` | lowercase | Function name verbatim |

**Key Rule**: NEVER mix literal constants with kindle constants. Literal constants have NO `${}` expansion and no runtime dependency — they're available immediately after sourcing. Kindle constants are computed at runtime during kindle.

---

### 2. ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS

#### Command Failures
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Every command that fails needs explicit handling
command || buc_die "Failed to do X — see ${z_stderr_file}"

# ✅ For predicates and capture functions (never die)
command || return 1

# ❌ NEVER: Silent failures
command 2>/dev/null
```

#### Stderr Capture (Never Suppress)
```bash
# ❌ NEVER suppress stderr
curl -sS "${z_url}" 2>/dev/null

# ✅ ALWAYS capture stderr to temp file, reference in die message
curl -sS "${z_url}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "Failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"

# In loops, use auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness
local z_index=0
while ...; do
  command 2>"${Z«PREFIX»_PREFIX}${z_index}_stderr.txt" || buc_die "..."
  z_index=$((z_index + 1))
done
```

#### Test Expressions
```bash
# ✅ ALWAYS use test command with || handling
test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "File not found"
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_die "Parameter required"
test "${z_var}" = "expected" || buc_warn "Unexpected"

# ❌ NEVER use test with command substitution
test $(echo "${z_val}" | grep pattern)  # ❌ Wrong

# ✅ Use exit-status predicates instead
echo "${z_val}" | grep -qE '^pattern$' || buc_die "Invalid"

# ✅ Or use case statement for patterns
case "${z_val}" in
  *pattern*) buc_log_args "Found pattern" ;;
  *) buc_die "Pattern not found" ;;
esac
```

#### Control Flow After Tests (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ✅ ALWAYS invert test and use || (BCG standard)
test "${z_val}" != "done" || break
test -n "${z_val}" || return 0
[[ "${z_line}" != *"pattern"* ]] || continue

# ❌ NEVER use test ... && for control flow
test "${z_val}" = "done" && break  # Wrong!

# ❌ NEVER use A && B || C pseudo-ternary
curl "${z_url}" && jq '.field' "${z_file}" || buc_die  # Wrong!

# ✅ Use explicit if/then/else instead
if curl "${z_url}"; then
  jq '.field' "${z_file}" || buc_die "jq failed"
else
  buc_die "curl failed"
fi
```

#### `set -e` Is Not Sufficient
```bash
# POSIX rule: set -e is SUPPRESSED inside if/while/||/&& test expressions
# This suppression PROPAGATES through the entire call tree

# ❌ WRONG: set -e suppressed, failures in some_function invisible
if some_function; then ...

# ✅ REQUIRED: Only _predicate functions in conditionals
if z«prefix»_ready_predicate; then ...

# ✅ REQUIRED: Regular functions use explicit error handling
some_function || buc_die "Failed"
```

#### Brace-Group vs Subshell for Error Handling
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Subshell exit only terminates subshell, not script
some_cmd || (echo "ERROR" >&2 && exit 1)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Brace-group stays in same process
some_cmd || { echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1; }
```

---

### 3. FUNCTION NAMING PATTERNS

| Type | Pattern | First Line | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Boilerplate kindle** | `z«prefix»_kindle` | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" \|\| buc_die` | Define kindle constants, set module state |
| **Boilerplate sentinel** | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | Guard all functions |
| **Boilerplate enforce** | `z«prefix»_enforce` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Validate regime (if applicable) |
| **Public functions** | `«prefix»_«command»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Always starts with sentinel check |
| **Internal helpers** | `z«prefix»_«name»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Prefix with `z` |
| **Predicate** | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_predicate` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Returns 0/1, no output, never dies |
| **Capture** | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_capture` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Returns stdout once or exit 1, no stderr |
| **Enroll** | `[z]«prefix»_[«scope»_]enroll` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Mutates rolls, returns via `z_«funcname»_«retval»` vars, kindle-only |
| **Recite** | `[z]«prefix»_«what»_recite` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Read-only roll access, outputs via echo |

**Special Function Rules**:
- **Predicate**: Never dies, status only (0=true, 1=false), no output, use in conditionals
- **Capture**: Clean error handling, single return via echo, no secrets on disk, use two-line pattern
- **Enroll**: Populate parallel arrays (rolls), return via variables not echo, kindle-only
- **Recite**: Read-only access to rolls, safe to call in `$()`

---

### 4. FILE I/O PATTERNS (ZEROES THEORY)

#### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Nested command substitution hides failures
z_result=$(echo $(complex_command) | process)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Temp files make failures visible
complex_command > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex failed"
process < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
read -r z_result < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "No result"

# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: Secrets use _capture functions, never temp files
```

**Temp file lifecycle**: Files under `BURD_TEMP_DIR` are **preserved after execution** for forensic debugging. Never delete them in module code.

#### File Reading Pattern
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Single-line builtin with validation
local -r z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"

# ❌ NEVER: Split declaration from $(<file) — unnecessary verbosity
local z_content
z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")
```

#### Capture Secrets (Never Disk)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Capture pattern for secrets
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"

# ❌ NEVER: Split declaration with capture
local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!
```

#### Line Reading from Files
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Guard handles missing trailing newline
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo"
done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"

# ✅ REQUIRED: Load-then-iterate pattern for loops (prevents stdin consumption)
local z_items=()
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  z_items+=("${z_line}")
done < "${z_temp_file}"

# ✅ Now iterate — stdin is free
for z_item in "${z_items[@]}"; do
  complex_function "${z_item}"
done
```

**Critical**: Never read file while loop body is open (stdin consumption). Load all items first, then iterate.

---

### 5. SUBSHELL USAGE

#### Error-Handling Blocks (FORBIDDEN)
```bash
# ❌ FORBIDDEN: Error blocks must use brace-group, not subshell
some_cmd || (echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1)

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use brace-group
some_cmd || { echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1; }
```

#### Legitimate Subshell Uses
```bash
# ✅ Isolation subshells (environment containment)
(
  source "${z_config_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source"
  «prefix»_operation || buc_die "Operation failed"
) || buc_die "Isolation subshell failed"

# ✅ Test execution (principled isolation)
# ✅ $() command substitution inside _capture functions
# ✅ Any sequence where environment mutations must not persist
```

---

### 6. LOCAL VARIABLES AND ASSIGNMENT

#### Variable Expansion (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Always use braced, quoted expansion
"${z_var}"              # Prevents word splitting, glob expansion
"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
"${HOME}/path"          # Use ${HOME} not ~

# ❌ NEVER use bare or unbraced expansion
$var                    # Unquoted — word splitting risk
"$var"                  # Unbraced — boundary ambiguity
${var}                  # Unquoted — glob expansion risk
~/path                  # Tilde in quotes doesn't expand
```

#### Local Variable Declaration
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: One declaration per line with explicit initialization
local z_name=""
local z_value="default"
local z_result=0

# ❌ NEVER: Multiple declarations on one line
local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use local -r for single-assignment locals (majority of locals)
local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
local -r z_content=$(<"${z_file}")

# EXCEPTIONS to local -r (these need mutable):
# 1. Loop counters
local z_index=0
z_index=$((z_index + 1))

# 2. Per-iteration synthesized variables
local z_manifest_file=""
for z_spec in "${z_specs[@]}"; do
  z_manifest_file="${Z_PREFIX}${z_index}_manifest.json"
done

# 3. Two-line capture pattern (readonly would lock empty value)
local z_token
z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed"

# 4. Accumulators and state-tracking flags
local z_any_failed=0
local z_current_group=""

# ❌ NEVER: local -i (silently coerces non-integers to 0)
local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0!

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use plain local with explicit validation
local z_count="${z_input}"
test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "Must be non-negative int"
```

#### Parameter Handling
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Defensive parameter pattern with defaults
local -r z_param1="${1:-}"
local -r z_param2="${2:-}"
test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "param1 is required"

# ❌ NEVER: Silent defaults for required parameters
local -r z_param="${1:-default}"  # Silently uses default if missing
```

---

### 7. READONLY PATTERNS (Three Scopes)

#### `readonly Z«PREFIX»_NAME` — Kindle Constants
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: All kindle assignments must be readonly
z«prefix»_kindle() {
  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "already kindled"
  
  # Literal constants
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_API_VERSION="v2"
  
  # Computed from other variables
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_LOCATION}${RBGC_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  
  # Composed from prior kindle assignments
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_ACCEPT_TYPES="${Z«PREFIX»_TYPE_A},${Z«PREFIX»_TYPE_B}"
  
  # MUST be last — sentinel checks this to know kindle is complete
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}

# ❌ NEVER: Kindle constant outside kindle
z«prefix»_setup() {
  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"  # VIOLATION
}
```

#### `local -r` — Default for Local Variables
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Majority of locals use local -r
local -r z_filepath="${1:-}"
local -r z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"

# Exceptions: Loop counters, per-iteration vars, two-line capture, accumulators
```

#### `readonly VAR` — Lock After Enforce (Regime Archetype)
```bash
# After z«prefix»_enforce succeeds, lock enrolled variables
readonly REGI_FIELD_ONE REGI_FIELD_TWO REGI_FIELD_THREE

# Build derived state from validated values after lock
Z«PREFIX»_DOCKER_ENV=("-e" "REGI_FIELD_ONE=${REGI_FIELD_ONE}")
```

---

### 8. CONDITIONAL LOGIC AND BRANCHING

#### Use `test` Not `[[ ]]`
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use test command for simple tests
test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "File not found"
test "${z_var}" = "expected" || buc_warn "Unexpected"
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_step "Running with zero-length parameter"

# ✅ EXCEPTION: Pattern matching ONLY
[[ "${z_filename}" =~ \.tar\.gz$ ]] && z_extension="tar.gz"

# ❌ NEVER: [[ ]] for simple tests
[[ -f "${z_file}" ]]        # Use: test -f
[[ "${z_var}" == "value" ]] # Use: test ... =

# ✅ REQUIRED: Use = not == for POSIX compliance
test "${z_var}" = "value"   # Not ==
```

#### Array Iteration (Under `set -u`)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Index iteration (always safe on empty arrays)
for z_i in "${!z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do
  echo "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[$z_i]}" || buc_die "Failed"
done

# ✅ REQUIRED: Guarded value iteration (guard prevents expansion)
if (( ${#z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]} )); then
  for z_val in "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do
    echo "${z_val}" || buc_die "Failed"
  done
fi

# ❌ NEVER: Unguarded value iteration on possibly-empty array
for z_val in "${z_«prefix»_name_roll[@]}"; do  # Crashes if empty!
  echo "${z_val}"
done
```

#### HERE-Strings (Not HERE-Docs)
```bash
# ✅ ALLOWED: Single-line HERE-string for piping variables
jq '.field' <<<"${z_json_data}"
grep "pattern" <<<"${z_content}"

# ❌ FORBIDDEN: Multi-line HERE-strings and HERE-docs
cat <<EOF > "${z_file}"
multi-line
content
EOF
```

---

### 9. INTERFACE CONTAMINATION DISCIPLINE

**One canonical form, no tolerances.**

```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: One canonical form for each parameter
«prefix»_command --option value

# ❌ NEVER: Accept alternatives
--option value      # Don't also accept -o
value               # Don't also accept positional
--OPTION value      # Don't also accept case-folded variants

# ✅ REQUIRED: Document canonical form only
buc_doc_param "--option" "Description of --option"

# ❌ NEVER: Undocumented defaults for required args
local -r z_param="${1:-default}"  # Silently fills missing required args

# ✅ REQUIRED: Explicit validation for required args
test -n "${z_param}" || buc_die "Parameter required"

# ❌ NEVER: Silent normalization
# Don't strip prefixes, fold case, or accept aliases
```

---

### 10. MISCELLANEOUS PATTERNS

#### Bare File Truncation
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: Looks like accidental deletion
> "${z_file}"

# ✅ REQUIRED: Explicit no-op makes intent unambiguous
: > "${z_file}"
```

#### Arithmetic
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use $((...)) bash builtin
z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))

# ❌ NEVER: Use expr or bc
z_result=$(expr $z_value \* 2 + 10)
```

#### String Manipulation
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Prefer parameter expansion (bash builtin)
"${z_path##*/}"                    # basename
"${z_path%/*}"                     # dirname
"${z_string//old/new}"             # sed 's/old/new/g'
"${z_string#prefix}"               # sed 's/^prefix//'
"${z_string%suffix}"               # sed 's/suffix$//'

# ✅ REQUIRED: When using %/%% with variables, quote inner expansion
"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"          # Correct
"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"          # Correct

# ❌ NEVER: Unquoted inner expansion
"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"            # Pattern matched as glob!
```

#### Bash Builtins Preferred Over External Tools
```bash
# ✅ String manipulation, arithmetic, parameter expansion use builtins
# ✅ This reduces process spawns, potential failures, and overhead
```

---

### 11. MODULE STRUCTURE

#### CLI as Module Gateway (CRITICAL)
```bash
# ❌ NEVER: External code calls z*_kindle directly
source "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_bottle.sh"
zrbob_kindle                          # VIOLATION

# ✅ REQUIRED: All external access goes through CLI
exec "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_cli.sh" rbob_start "$@"

# External code = entry points, workbenches, testbenches, dispatchers
# They must use CLI scripts, not direct kindle calls
```

#### CLI Template
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: CLI header
set -euo pipefail
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

z«prefix»_furnish() {
  # Document env vars actually used
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR" "..."
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
  
  # Source dependencies
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/«prefix»_«name».sh"
  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed"
  
  z«prefix»_kindle
  z«prefix»_enforce  # If regime archetype
}

buc_execute «prefix»_ "description" z«prefix»_furnish "$@"
```

#### Implementation Module Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Copyright

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module multiply sourced"
Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no $)
«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions

z«prefix»_kindle() {
  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Already kindled"
  
  # Validate BURD variables used
  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  
  # Kindle constants
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"
  readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}

z«prefix»_sentinel() {
  test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Not kindled"
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions

«prefix»_«command»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  local -r z_param1="${1:-}"
  
  buc_doc_brief "Description"
  buc_doc_param "arg" "description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "Param required"
  buc_step "User-visible milestone"
  buc_log_args "Detailed progress"
  
  command || buc_die "Failed"
  buc_success "Success message"
}
```

---

### 12. DOCUMENTATION AND VISIBILITY

#### Never Use Comments for Runtime Info
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Use runtime visibility functions
buc_step "Fetching manifest from registry"         # User milestones
buc_log_args "Processing platform linux/amd64"     # Variable details
jq '.response' file.json | buc_log_pipe            # Pipeline output

# ❌ NEVER: Use comments for runtime information
# Building JWT header      ❌ Wrong
buc_log_args "Building JWT header"  ✅ Correct

# ❌ NEVER: Commit-message comments (narrate change, not code)
# Added qualification subsystem for shellcheck support  ❌ Wrong
buc_code "command"  ✅ Show commands with buc_code
```

#### Function Documentation (buc_doc_*)
```bash
# ✅ REQUIRED: Document all public functions
«prefix»_«command»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  
  buc_doc_brief "One-line description"
  buc_doc_param "required_arg" "Description"
  buc_doc_oparm "optional_arg" "Description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  # Implementation
}

# ✅ RECOMMENDED: Document internal functions too
z«prefix»_«name»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel
  
  buc_doc_brief "One-line description"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0
  
  # Implementation
}
```

---

### 13. PROHIBITED CONSTRUCTS

| Construct | Why Forbidden | Alternative |
|---|---|---|
| **Inline shellcheck directives** | All suppression in busc_shellcheckrc | Configure centrally |
| **Bash 4+ features** | Bash 3.2 compatibility | Use 3.2-compatible alternatives |
| **Associative arrays** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use parallel arrays with indexes |
| **`**` globbing** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use find or explicit patterns |
| **readarray/mapfile** | Not in bash 3.2 | Use while/read loop |
| **HERE-docs** | Breaks indentation | Use echo sequences or temp files |
| **`eval` for assignment** | Quoting hazards, injection | Use `printf -v` or `${!var}` |
| **`test $(command)`** | Crashes when command returns nothing | Use `command -q` or `case` |
| **`test ... && break/return`** | Relies on set -e exemption hazard | Invert test, use `\|\|` |
| **Regular functions in conditionals** | set -e suppressed entire call tree | Only `_predicate` in if/while |
| **Subshell error handling** | exit only kills subshell | Use brace-group `{ ... }` |

---

### 14. QUICK VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

- [ ] All variables use `"${var}"` pattern (braced, quoted)
- [ ] All kindle constants are `readonly`
- [ ] Mutable kindle state uses lowercase `z_` prefix
- [ ] Every command that fails has `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`
- [ ] Stderr redirected to temp file, path in die message
- [ ] Control flow uses `||` not `&&` with tests
- [ ] Only `_predicate` functions in `if`/`while` conditions
- [ ] Error blocks use `{ ... }` not `( ... )`
- [ ] No bash 4+ features (associative arrays, `**`, readarray)
- [ ] No bare `> file` truncation (use `: > file`)
- [ ] No inline shellcheck directives
- [ ] No commit-message comments
- [ ] File reads validated for non-empty
- [ ] Two-line capture pattern for substitutions
- [ ] Temp files preserved for forensics (never deleted)

This is the complete BCG compliance framework for this project.

buc_step "Logging into GAR"
  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_gar_host}" \
    || buc_die "GAR authentication failed"

  # Pull upstream image locally (for SBOM generation and digest verification)
  buc_step "Pulling bind image from upstream"
  buc_info "Source: ${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}"
  docker pull "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}" || buc_die "Failed to pull bind image: ${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}"

  # Copy all platforms from upstream to GAR using buildx imagetools
  local -r z_build_ts="b$(date -u +'%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')"
  local -r z_consecration="${z_mirror_ts}-${z_build_ts}"
  local -r z_image_tag="${z_consecration}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
  local -r z_image_ref="${z_gar_base}/${RBRV_SIGIL}:${z_image_tag}"

  buc_step "Mirroring to GAR (all platforms)"
  buc_info "Target: ${z_image_ref}"
  docker buildx imagetools create \
    --tag "${z_image_ref}" \
    "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to mirror bind image to GAR"

  buc_info "Image mirrored: ${z_image_ref}"

  # Capture git metadata
  buc_step "Capturing git metadata"
  local z_git_commit=""
  local z_git_branch=""
  z_git_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD) || buc_die "Failed to get commit SHA"
  z_git_branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) || buc_die "Failed to get branch"
  local z_git_remote_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_mirror_git_remote.txt"
  git remote > "${z_git_remote_file}" || buc_die "Failed to list git remotes"
  local z_git_remote=""
  z_git_remote=$(head -1 "${z_git_remote_file}")
  test -n "${z_git_remote}" || buc_die "No git remotes found"
  local z_git_repo_url=""
  z_git_repo_url=$(git config --get "remote.${z_git_remote}.url") || buc_die "Failed to get repo URL"
  local z_git_repo="${z_git_repo_url#*://*/}"
  z_git_repo="${z_git_repo%.git}"

  # Generate build_info.json (bind-specific)
  buc_step "Generating mirror metadata"
  local -r z_build_info_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/build_info.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg mode          "bind" \
    --arg moniker       "${RBRV_SIGIL}" \
    --arg source_image  "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}" \
    --arg consecration  "${z_consecration}" \
    --arg mirror_ts     "${z_mirror_ts}" \
    --arg build_ts      "${z_build_ts}" \
    --arg git_repo      "${z_git_repo}" \
    --arg git_branch    "${z_git_branch}" \
    --arg git_commit    "${z_git_commit}" \
    --arg image_uri     "${z_image_ref}" \
    '{
      mode: $mode,
      moniker: $moniker,
      source: {
        image_ref: $source_image,
        vessel_mode: "bind"
      },
      build: {
        timestamp: $build_ts,
        inscribe_timestamp: $mirror_ts,
        consecration: $consecration
      },
      git: {
        repo: $git_repo,
        branch: $git_branch,
        commit: $git_commit
      },
      image: {
        uri: $image_uri
      },
      trust: {

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry - GCB image creation and GAR deletion

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBF_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbf multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBF_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbf_*)

zrbf_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBF_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbf already kindled"

  # Validate environment
  zburd_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Check required GCB/GAR environment variables'
  zrbgc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify service account files'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'Module Variables (ZRBF_*)'
  readonly ZRBF_GCB_API_BASE="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBF_GAR_API_BASE="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBF_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-build/builds"

  readonly ZRBF_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL="${ZRBF_GCB_API_BASE}/projects/${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/builds"
  readonly ZRBF_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  buc_log_args 'Trigger dispatch endpoints'
  readonly ZRBF_TRIGGERS_URL="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/triggers"

  buc_log_args 'Registry API endpoints for delete'
  readonly ZRBF_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  readonly ZRBF_REGISTRY_PATH="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBF_REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${ZRBF_REGISTRY_HOST}/v2/${ZRBF_REGISTRY_PATH}"

  buc_log_args 'Media types for delete operation'
  readonly ZRBF_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJ files in same Tools directory as this implementation'
  # Acronym: rbgjb = Recipe Bottle Google Json Build (step scripts in rbgjb/ dir)
  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBF_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjb"
  test -d "${ZRBF_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJB steps directory not found: ${ZRBF_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJV vouch step scripts (same Tools directory)'
  # Acronym: rbgjv = Recipe Bottle Google Json Vouch (step scripts in rbgjv/ dir)
  readonly ZRBF_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjv"
  test -d "${ZRBF_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJV steps directory not found: ${ZRBF_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'Define temp files for build operations'
  readonly ZRBF_BUILD_ID_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_build_id.txt"
  readonly ZRBF_BUILD_STATUS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_build_status.json"
  readonly ZRBF_BUILD_RUBRIC_LS="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_rubric_ls_remote.txt"
  readonly ZRBF_BUILD_TRIGGER_BODY="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_trigger_run_body.json"

  buc_log_args 'Define git info file (used by inscribe -> stitch)'
  readonly ZRBF_GIT_INFO_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_git_info.json"

  buc_log_args 'Define validation files'
  readonly ZRBF_STATUS_CHECK_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_status_check.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation files'
  readonly ZRBF_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_delete_"
  readonly ZRBF_TOKEN_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_token.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define vouch operation file prefix (postfixed per step id)'
  readonly ZRBF_VOUCH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_vouch_"

  buc_log_args 'Vessel-related files'
  readonly ZRBF_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_vessel_sigil.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define stitch operation file prefix (postfixed per step id)'
  readonly ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_stitch_"

  buc_log_args 'Define inscribe operation files'
  readonly ZRBF_INSCRIBE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbf_inscribe_"
  readonly ZRBF_INSCRIBE_CLONE_DIR="${RBGC_RUBRIC_CLONE_DIR}"
  readonly ZRBF_INSCRIBE_STALENESS_SEC=86400

  buc_log_args 'Define output files (BURD_OUTPUT_DIR — persists after dispatch)'
  readonly ZRBF_OUTPUT_VESSEL_DIR="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/rbf_vessel_dir.txt"

  buc_log_args 'For now lets double check these'
  test -n "${RBRG_ORAS_IMAGE_REF:-}"   || buc_die "RBRG_ORAS_IMAGE_REF not set"

  readonly ZRBF_KINDLED=1
}

zrbf_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBF_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbf not kindled - call zrbf_kindle first"
}

# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
# Args: token mode
#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
zrbf_quota_preflight() {
  zrbf_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_mode="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbf_quota_preflight: token required"
  test -n "${z_mode}"  || buc_die "zrbf_quota_preflight: mode required (gate|advisory)"

  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
  case "${z_vcpus}" in
    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac

  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"

  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"

  buc_step "Checking concurrent build quota"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"

  local z_code=""
  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
    return 0
  fi

  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
  rbgu_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRR_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
    || true

  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
  local z_limit=""
  z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
    z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
  fi

  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
    return 0
  fi

  # -1 means unlimited
  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
    return 0
  fi

  # Compute max concurrent builds
  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))

  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"

  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
    if test "${z_mode}" = "gate"; then
      buc_die "Quota preflight failed -- review capacity settings above"
    fi
  else
    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
  fi
}

zrbf_stitch_build_json() {
  zrbf_sentinel

  local -r z_output_path="${1:?Output path required}"

  buc_log_args "Stitching trigger-compatible build JSON to ${z_output_path}"

  # Preconditions: vessel loaded and git state captured
  test -s "${ZRBF_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}" || buc_die "Vessel not loaded — call zrbf_load_vessel first"
  test -s "${ZRBF_GIT_INFO_FILE}"     || buc_die "Git info not captured — ensure git metadata is captured before stitch"

  buc_log_args 'Read vessel state for substitutions'
  local -r z_sigil=$(<"${ZRBF_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_sigil}" || buc_die "Empty vessel sigil"
  local -r z_dockerfile_name="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE##*/}"
  local -r z_platforms="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}"

  # Platform count detection
  local z_platform_count=0
  local z_remaining_count="${z_platforms}"
  local z_p_count=""
  while test -n "${z_remaining_count}"; do
    z_p_count="${z_remaining_count%%,*}"
    z_platform_count=$((z_platform_count + 1))
    test "${z_remaining_count}" != "${z_p_count}" || break
    z_remaining_count="${z_remaining_count#*,}"
  done
  buc_log_args "Vessel platforms: ${z_platform_count} (${z_platforms})"

  buc_log_args 'Extract git state for substitutions'
  local -r z_stitch_git_commit_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}git_commit.txt"
  local -r z_stitch_git_branch_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}git_branch.txt"
  local -r z_stitch_git_repo_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}git_repo.txt"

  jq -r '.commit' "${ZRBF_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_commit_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git commit from info file"
  jq -r '.branch' "${ZRBF_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_branch_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git branch from info file"
  jq -r '.repo'   "${ZRBF_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_repo_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git repo from info file"

  local -r z_git_commit=$(<"${z_stitch_git_commit_file}")
  local -r z_git_branch=$(<"${z_stitch_git_branch_file}")
  local -r z_git_repo=$(<"${z_stitch_git_repo_file}")

  test -n "${z_git_commit}" || buc_die "Git commit is empty"
  test -n "${z_git_branch}" || buc_die "Git branch is empty"
  test -n "${z_git_repo}"   || buc_die "Git repo is empty"

  # Build strategy: compare vessel platforms against runner platform
  # If platforms exactly match the runner, no QEMU emulation is needed (native build).
  # Any difference (multi-platform or non-native single-platform) requires binfmt.
  local z_needs_binfmt="true"
  if test "${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}" = "${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM}"; then
    z_needs_binfmt="false"
  fi

  local z_build_strategy=""
  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
    z_build_strategy="emulated multi-platform via QEMU (${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,})"
    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 included"
  else
    z_build_strategy="native single-platform (${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM})"
    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 excluded"
  fi

  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
  # Entrypoint 'bash' uses args ["-lc", script], 'sh' uses ["-c", script]
  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
  # Pipeline: buildx --push → per-platform pullback → SLSA provenance via images: field
  local z_step_defs=(
    "rbgjb01-derive-tag-base.sh|${RBRG_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF}|bash|derive-tag-base"
  )
  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
    z_step_defs+=("rbgjb02-qemu-binfmt.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|qemu-binfmt")
  fi
  z_step_defs+=(
    "rbgjb03-buildx-push-multi.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|buildx-push-multi"
    "rbgjb04-per-platform-pullback.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|per-platform-pullback"
    "rbgjb05-push-per-platform.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|push-per-platform"
    "rbgjb06-syft-per-platform.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|syft-per-platform"
    "rbgjb07-build-info-per-platform.sh|${RBRG_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF}|sh|build-info-per-platform"
    "rbgjb08-buildx-push-about.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|buildx-push-about"
    "rbgjb09-imagetools-create.sh|${RBRG_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}|bash|imagetools-create"
  )

  # Compute platform suffixes (used in images: field and substitutions)
  # Always computed: linux/amd64 → -amd64, linux/arm64 → -arm64, linux/arm/v7 → -armv7
  local z_platform_suffixes=""
  local z_platform_suffixes_csv=""
  local z_remaining_plats="${z_platforms}"
  local z_plat=""
  local z_suffix=""
  while test -n "${z_remaining_plats}"; do
    z_plat="${z_remaining_plats%%,*}"
    # Strip linux/ prefix, collapse remaining slashes: linux/arm/v7 → armv7
    z_suffix="${z_plat#linux/}"
    z_suffix="${z_suffix//\//}"
    z_suffix="-${z_suffix}"
    if test -n "${z_platform_suffixes}"; then
      z_platform_suffixes="${z_platform_suffixes},${z_suffix}"
    else
      z_platform_suffixes="${z_suffix}"
    fi
    test "${z_remaining_plats}" != "${z_plat}" || break
    z_remaining_plats="${z_remaining_plats#*,}"
  done
  z_platform_suffixes_csv="${z_platform_suffixes}"
  buc_log_args "Platform suffixes: ${z_platform_suffixes_csv}"

  local z_def=""
  local z_script=""
  local z_builder=""
  local z_entrypoint=""
  local z_id=""
  local z_script_path=""
  local z_body=""
  local z_arg_flag=""
  local z_body_file=""
  local z_escaped_file=""
  local z_steps_file=""
  local z_accumulator_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}steps.json"

  buc_log_args "Initializing empty steps array"
  echo "[]" > "${z_accumulator_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize steps JSON"

  for z_def in "${z_step_defs[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_script z_builder z_entrypoint z_id <<< "${z_def}"
    z_script_path="${ZRBF_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}/${z_script}"
    z_body_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_body.txt"
    z_escaped_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_escaped.txt"
    z_steps_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}${z_id}_steps.json"

    test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Step script not found: ${z_script_path}"

    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_id} (skip shebang, comments pass through)"
    tail -n +2 "${z_script_path}" > "${z_body_file}" || buc_die "Failed to read step script: ${z_script_path}"
    z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
    test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty script body: ${z_script_path}"

    buc_log_args "Baking pinned image refs and build strategy into script text"
    z_body="${z_body//\$\{RBRG_SYFT_IMAGE_REF\}/${RBRG_SYFT_IMAGE_REF}}"
    z_body="${z_body//\$\{RBRG_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF\}/${RBRG_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF}}"
    z_body="${z_body//\$\{ZRBF_BUILD_STRATEGY\}/${z_build_strategy}}"

    buc_log_args "Escaping dollars for Cloud Build, preserving RBGY substitutions"
    printf '%s' "${z_body}" | sed 's/\$/\$\$/g; s/\$\${_RBGY_/${_RBGY_/g' \
      > "${z_escaped_file}" || buc_die "Failed to escape script body for ${z_id}"

    case "${z_entrypoint}" in
      bash) z_entrypoint="/bin/bash"; z_arg_flag="-lc" ;;
      sh)   z_entrypoint="/bin/sh";   z_arg_flag="-c" ;;
      *)    buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_entrypoint}" ;;
    esac

    buc_log_args "Appending step ${z_id} to JSON array"
    jq \
      --arg name "${z_builder}" \
      --arg id "${z_id}" \
      --arg ep "${z_entrypoint}" \
      --arg flag "${z_arg_flag}" \
      --arg dir "${z_sigil}" \
      --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, entrypoint: $ep, dir: $dir, args: [$flag, $script]}]' \
      "${z_accumulator_file}" > "${z_steps_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append step ${z_id} to JSON"
    mv "${z_steps_file}" "${z_accumulator_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update steps JSON for ${z_id}"
  done

  # Compose complete trigger-compatible Build resource
  # Steps from accumulator, substitutions from module state, options/timeout from RBRR
  # _RBGY_RUBRIC_REPO, _RBGY_RUBRIC_COMMIT, _RBGY_INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP, _RBGY_GIT_COMMIT
  # are placeholders in the stitched output; inscribe fills them in the rubric repo clone
  buc_log_args "Composing complete trigger-compatible Build resource"
  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}build.json"

  # images: field uses inscribe-time-predictable alias tags for SLSA provenance.
  # CB pushes these after all steps complete, generating provenance attestations
  # keyed to image digests. The consecration-tagged copies (pushed in step 05)
  # share the same digests, so provenance applies to them too.
  # Alias tags are ephemeral (overwritten on re-conjure); consecration tags persist.

  # Build images: array — one entry per platform using inscribe-time alias tag
  local z_images_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}images.json"
  local z_image_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${z_sigil}"
  local z_remaining_suffixes="${z_platform_suffixes_csv}"
  local z_img_suffix=""
  echo "[]" > "${z_images_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize images JSON"
  while test -n "${z_remaining_suffixes}"; do
    z_img_suffix="${z_remaining_suffixes%%,*}"
    jq --arg uri "${z_image_base}:__INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP__-image${z_img_suffix}" \
      '. + [$uri]' "${z_images_file}" > "${z_images_file}.tmp" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append image URI"
    mv "${z_images_file}.tmp" "${z_images_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update images JSON"
    test "${z_remaining_suffixes}" != "${z_img_suffix}" || break
    z_remaining_suffixes="${z_remaining_suffixes#*,}"
  done

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps  "${z_accumulator_file}" \
    --slurpfile zjq_images "${z_images_file}" \
    --arg zjq_dockerfile     "${z_dockerfile_name}" \
    --arg zjq_moniker        "${z_sigil}" \
    --arg zjq_platforms      "${z_platforms}" \
    --arg zjq_platform_suffixes "${z_platform_suffixes_csv}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_location   "${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_project    "${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_repository "${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}" \
    --arg zjq_git_commit     "__INSCRIBE_GIT_COMMIT__" \
    --arg zjq_git_branch     "${z_git_branch}" \
    --arg zjq_git_repo       "${z_git_repo}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_host_suffix  "${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_ark_suffix_image "${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}" \
    --arg zjq_ark_suffix_about "${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_ABOUT}" \
    --arg zjq_rubric_repo      "__INSCRIBE_RUBRIC_REPO__" \
    --arg zjq_rubric_commit    "__INSCRIBE_RUBRIC_COMMIT__" \
    --arg zjq_inscribe_ts      "__INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP__" \
    --arg zjq_pool   "${RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL}" \
    --arg zjq_timeout "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
    '{
      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
      images: $zjq_images[0],
      substitutions: {
        _RBGY_DOCKERFILE:          $zjq_dockerfile,
        _RBGY_MONIKER:             $zjq_moniker,
        _RBGY_PLATFORMS:           $zjq_platforms,
        _RBGY_PLATFORM_SUFFIXES:   $zjq_platform_suffixes,
        _RBGY_GAR_LOCATION:        $zjq_gar_location,
        _RBGY_GAR_PROJECT:         $zjq_gar_project,
        _RBGY_GAR_REPOSITORY:      $zjq_gar_repository,
        _RBGY_GIT_COMMIT:          $zjq_git_commit,
        _RBGY_GIT_BRANCH:          $zjq_git_branch,
        _RBGY_GIT_REPO:            $zjq_git_repo,
        _RBGY_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX:     $zjq_gar_host_suffix,
        _RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE:    $zjq_ark_suffix_image,
        _RBGY_ARK_SUFFIX_ABOUT:    $zjq_ark_suffix_about,
        _RBGY_RUBRIC_REPO:         $zjq_rubric_repo,
        _RBGY_RUBRIC_COMMIT:       $zjq_rubric_commit,
        _RBGY_INSCRIBE_TIMESTAMP:  $zjq_inscribe_ts
      },
      options: {
        requestedVerifyOption: "VERIFIED",
        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
        pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
      },
      timeout: $zjq_timeout
    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose build JSON"

  mv "${z_build_file}" "${z_output_path}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write final stitched build JSON to ${z_output_path}"

  buc_log_args "Stitched ${#z_step_defs[@]} steps to ${z_output_path}"
}

zrbf_load_vessel() {
  zrbf_sentinel

  local z_vessel_dir="$1"

  buc_log_args 'Validate vessel directory exists'
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  buc_log_args 'Check for rbrv.env file'
  local z_vessel_env="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  test -f "${z_vessel_env}" || buc_die "Vessel configuration not found: ${z_vessel_env}"

  buc_log_args 'Source vessel configuration'
  source "${z_vessel_env}" || buc_die "Failed to source vessel config: ${z_vessel_env}"

  buc_log_args 'Validate vessel directory matches sigil'
  local z_vessel_dir_clean="${z_vessel_dir%/}"  # Strip any trailing slash
  local z_dir_name="${z_vessel_dir_clean##*/}"  # Extract directory name
  buc_log_args "  z_vessel_dir = ${z_vessel_dir}"
  buc_log_args "  z_dir_name   = ${z_dir_name}"
  test "${z_dir_name}" = "${RBRV_SIGIL}" || buc_die "Vessel sigil '${RBRV_SIGIL}' does not match directory name '${z_dir_name}'"

  buc_log_args 'Validate vessel path matches expected pattern'
  local z_expected_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRV_SIGIL}"
  local z_vessel_realpath=""
  z_vessel_realpath=$(cd "${z_vessel_dir}" && pwd) || buc_die "Failed to resolve vessel directory path"
  local z_expected_realpath=""
  z_expected_realpath=$(cd "${z_expected_vessel_dir}" && pwd) || buc_die "Failed to resolve expected vessel path"
  test "${z_vessel_realpath}" = "${z_expected_realpath}" || buc_die "Vessel directory '${z_vessel_dir}' does not match expected location '${z_expected_vessel_dir}'"

  buc_log_args 'Store loaded vessel info for use by commands'
  echo "${RBRV_SIGIL}" > "${ZRBF_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to store vessel sigil"

  buc_info "Loaded vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

zrbf_wait_build_completion() {
  zrbf_sentinel

  local z_max_polls="${1:?zrbf_wait_build_completion: max_polls required}"

  buc_step 'Waiting for build completion'

  local z_build_id=""
  z_build_id=$(<"${ZRBF_BUILD_ID_FILE}") || buc_die "No build ID found"
  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID file empty"

  buc_log_args 'Get fresh token for polling'
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get GCB OAuth token"

  local z_status="PENDING"
  local z_polls=0
  local z_consecutive_failures=0
  local z_max_consecutive_failures=3
  local z_err_check_file="${ZRBF_STITCH_PREFIX}poll_err_check.txt"

  while true; do
    case "${z_status}" in PENDING|QUEUED|WORKING) : ;; *) break;; esac
    sleep 5

    z_polls=$((z_polls + 1))
    test "${z_polls}" -le "${z_max_polls}" || buc_die "Build timeout after ${z_max_polls} polls"

    buc_log_args "Fetch build status (poll ${z_polls}/${z_max_polls})"
    curl -s                                                \
         --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
         --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"             \
         -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"             \
         "${ZRBF_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}/${z_build_id}"    \
         > "${ZRBF_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}"
    if test $? -ne 0; then
      z_consecutive_failures=$((z_consecutive_failures + 1))
      buc_warn "Curl failed (${z_consecutive_failures}/${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive)"
      test ${z_consecutive_failures} -ge ${z_max_consecutive_failures} \
        && buc_die "Failed to get build status after ${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive failures"
      continue
    fi

    # Validate response is non-empty
    if ! test -s "${ZRBF_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}"; then
      z_consecutive_failures=$((z_consecutive_failures + 1))
      buc_warn "Empty response (${z_consecutive_failures}/${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive)"
      test ${z_consecutive_failures} -ge ${z_max_consecutive_failures} \
        && buc_die "Empty build status after ${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive failures"
      continue
    fi

    # Check for HTTP error responses (401/403/etc) — write to temp file, no subshell
    jq -r '.error.code // empty' "${ZRBF_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" > "${z_err_check_file}" 2>/dev/null
    if test -s "${z_err_check_file}"; then
      z_consecutive_failures=$((z_consecutive_failures + 1))
      buc_warn "HTTP error $(<"${z_err_check_file}") (${z_consecutive_failures}/${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive)"
      test ${z_consecutive_failures} -ge ${z_max_consecutive_failures} \
        && buc_die "HTTP errors after ${z_max_consecutive_failures} consecutive failures"
      continue
    fi

    # Successful response — reset failure counter
    z_consecutive_failures=0

    jq -r '.status' "${ZRBF_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" > "${ZRBF_STATUS_CHECK_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to extract status"
